corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: The model architecture, shown in figure , is a slight variant of the CNN architecture of .
In the multichannel architecture, illustrated in figure 1, each filter is applied to both channels and the results are added to calculate c i in equation ( ).

We had initially hoped that the multichannel architecture would prevent overfitting (by ensuring that the learned vectors do not deviate too far from the original values) and thus work better than the single channel model, especially on smaller datasets.

4 • SST-2: Same as SST-1 but with neutral reviews removed and binary labels. • Subj: Subjectivity dataset where the task is to classify a sentence as being subjective or objective ). • TREC: TREC question dataset-task involves classifying a question into 6 question types (whether the question is about person, location, numeric information, etc.) ).
5 6 ∼ liub/FBS/sentiment-analysis.html • MPQA: Opinion polarity detection subtask of the MPQA dataset .
The model is otherwise equivalent to the single channel architecture.
We report on a series of experiments with convolutional neural networks (CNN) trained on top of pre-trained word vectors for sentence-level classification tasks.
We show that a simple CNN with little hyperparameter tuning and static vectors achieves excellent results on multiple benchmarks.
We additionally propose a simple modification to the architecture to allow for the use of both task-specific and static vectors.
We additionally propose a simple modification to the architecture to allow for the use of both task-specific and static vectors.
The CNN models discussed herein improve upon the state of the art on 4 out of 7 tasks, which include sentiment analysis and question classification.
In the present work, we train a simple CNN with one layer of convolution on top of word vectors obtained from an unsupervised neural language model.

Despite little tuning of hyperparameters, this simple model achieves excellent results on multiple benchmarks, suggesting that the pre-trained vectors are 'universal' feature extractors that can be utilized for various classification tasks.
We finally describe a simple modification to the architecture to allow for the use of both pre-trained and task-specific vectors by having multiple channels.

In order to disentangle the effect of the above variations versus other random factors, we eliminate other sources of randomness-CV-fold assignment, initialization of unknown word vectors, initialization of CNN parameters-by keeping them uniform within each dataset.

In the present work we have described a series of experiments with convolutional neural networks built on top of word2vec.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
